# musicguessr

> Open-source, platform-agnostic web companion for the Hitster card game. Play any Hitster card with YouTube, Spotify, or Apple Music — no subscription lock-in.

## What is musicguessr?

musicguessr lets players scan QR codes from Hitster music quiz cards and instantly play the corresponding track in their browser using whichever music service they have:

- **YouTube** — always available, no account required
- **Spotify** — via Web Playback SDK (requires Premium)
- **Apple Music** — via MusicKit JS (requires subscription)

The game: players listen to a song snippet, then guess which year it was released. Cards are placed on a timeline in chronological order. The player with the most correctly placed cards wins.

## Key Features

- **QR code scanner** — point your camera at any Hitster card to identify the track
- **Multi-provider playback** — YouTube, Spotify, Apple Music
- **Custom deck creator** — build your own music quiz deck from YouTube videos or playlists
- **Playlist import** — import an entire YouTube playlist as a deck in one click
- **Shareable decks** — share a link or QR code so friends can play your custom deck
- **PWA** — installable on mobile, works offline (cached decks)
- **No user accounts** — fully stateless, no login required

## How it works

1. Go to musicguessr and choose your music provider (YouTube / Spotify / Apple Music)
2. Scan a QR code from a Hitster card using your camera
3. Tap to play — the track starts in your browser
4. Guess the year, then tap to reveal the answer
5. Optionally create and share your own custom music quiz decks

## Custom Decks

Users can create custom decks at `/create-deck`:
- Add YouTube video URLs one by one, or import an entire YouTube playlist
- Each card stores the YouTube video ID, title, artist, and year
- Decks are shareable via a short link and QR code
- Decks expire after a configurable TTL (1 week to 1 year)

## Technical Details

- Frontend: Angular 19 PWA (TypeScript, standalone components, signals)
- Backend: Go 1.23 REST API (stdlib only, no framework)
- Storage: local filesystem or S3-compatible object storage (Cloudflare R2)
- No user authentication, no cookies, no tracking
- Open source — MIT license

## Supported Platforms

- Modern browsers: Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge
- Mobile: iOS Safari, Android Chrome (QR scanner requires camera permission)
- iOS autoplay: handled via tap-to-play gesture gate

## Links

- Home: /
- Create custom deck: /create-deck
- My decks: /deck
- GitHub: https://github.com/musicguessr
